title: keckiella ternata
intro: Keckiella ternata, also known as whorled penstemon, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the plantain family and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. Keckiella ternata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
